title: eriogonum rosense var. beatleyae
intro: Eriogonum rosense var. beatleyae, also known as Beatley's buckwheat, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the southwestern United States. As a member of the buckwheat family, it has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, derived from its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Eriogonum rosense var. beatleyae include its potential to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas and infusions, which can be made by steeping the leaves in hot water, as well as tinctures and salves, which can be used topically to apply its antiseptic properties.
